_comment: Curated IPv6 caveats committed to the repo so caveats.db is reproducible without the external archived-kb bug file. Ingested by tools/migrate/populate_caveats.py. Source: Cisco public bug IDs (agent-cisco-kb bug-details.json).

caveats.headline: Kernel panic due to "Fatal Module Error" after ND ISSU on N9K-C9348GC-FXP
caveats.description: After a Non-Disruptive (ND) ISSU upgrade to an impacted release, an N9K-C9348GC-FXP reloads ~2 minutes later with 'Reset Requested due to Fatal Module Error' (visible in 'show system reset-reason' / 'show logging onboard internal reset-reason'). Workaround: use the disruptive/normal upgrade procedure instead of ND ISSU.
caveats.keywords: ipv6 nd issu non-disruptive upgrade kernel panic fatal module error reload reset-reason

caveats.headline: Configuring overlapping IPv6 address with different mask on L3 interface does not throw an error
caveats.description: On Nexus 9000, configuring an IPv6 address that overlaps subnet-wise (different prefix length, e.g. 2001:db0::1/72 then /73) on two different L3 interfaces produces no warning, unlike IPv4 which warns on overlap. Only an exact duplicate address is rejected. Operators can silently misconfigure overlapping IPv6 subnets.
caveats.keywords: ipv6 overlapping address subnet no error warning misconfiguration l3 interface svi loopback

caveats.headline: Memory leak on FED due to IPv6 Source Guard
caveats.description: IPv6 Source Guard (device-tracking / RSC_SI library) leaks memory on the FED process: entries are allocated but not cleaned up, growing FED memory utilization over time. Verify with 'show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource utilization | i RSC_SI' and 'show device-tracking database'. Workaround: disable IPv6 Source Guard.
caveats.keywords: ipv6 source guard memory leak fed rsc_si device-tracking catalyst

caveats.headline: DHCPv6 RELAY-REPLY packet is being dropped
caveats.description: A Catalyst 9500 acting as a DHCPv6 relay sends RELAY-FORWARD to the server and the server's RELAY-REPLY arrives at the 9500 (visible in an on-box capture), but the relay does not forward the unwrapped Reply to the client VLAN, so end clients never receive DHCPv6 configuration (e.g. DNS via Information-Request). Affects 16.11.1 and 16.12.1 through 16.12.3a. Workaround: none documented.
caveats.keywords: dhcpv6 relay relay-reply relay-forward dropped not forwarded client cat9500 information-request

caveats.headline: NETCONF incorrectly activates IPv4 address-family for an IPv6 BGP peer
caveats.description: When activating a second IPv6 BGP peer via NETCONF/YANG, IOS-XE incorrectly emits 'neighbor <ipv6> activate' under address-family ipv4 for the new peer (instead of only under address-family ipv6), unlike the first peer which is correctly deactivated under IPv4. Results in an unintended IPv4 AF activation for an IPv6-only neighbor. Workaround: configure/correct the neighbor activation via CLI.
caveats.keywords: ipv6 bgp netconf yang address-family ipv4 activate neighbor incorrect
